framework: A path qualifies only if you can point at companies hiring for it or a role title that demonstrably exists - not a blog asserting it matters. Ranked by strength of that evidence, not by how often the skill is praised. Curated by hand; this file is editorial, which is why it is NOT inside <role>.json (that bundle is DOL-derived only).

paths.path: Own something in production, not just build it
paths.what_it_is: Run what you wrote: on-call, incidents, real users, and the boring maintenance after launch.
paths.why_it_signals: Top employers explicitly screen out engineers who have only built prototypes or MVPs without owning production systems. It is the single most-cited gap.

paths.what_it_is: Reason about partitions, consistency tradeoffs and failure modes, not just features.
paths.why_it_signals: Distributed Systems Engineer is a posted title with its own salary band, and system-design interviews on distributed failure are a standard filter.

paths.path: Work across the stack, not one technology
paths.what_it_is: Move through the backend platform rather than staying inside a single framework or service.
paths.why_it_signals: Companies report avoiding candidates who specialise in one technology area and will not work across the broader platform.
